title: Kamakhya Temple
tagline: Kamakhya Temple — spiritual town of Assam

overview.short: Kamakhya Temple (Assamese: kamakhya mondir) is a Hindu temple at Nilachal hills in Guwahati, Assam is one of the oldest and most revered centres of Tantric practices, dedicated to
overview.description: Kamakhya Temple (Assamese: kamakhya mondir) is a Hindu temple at Nilachal hills in Guwahati, Assam is one of the oldest and most revered centres of Tantric practices, dedicated to the goddess Kamakhya. The temple is the center of the Kulachara Tantra Marga and the site of the Ambubachi Mela, an annual festival that celebrates the menstruation of the goddess. Structurally, the temple is dated to the 8th-9th century with many subsequent rebuildings—and the final hybrid architecture defines a local style called Nilachal. It is also one among the oldest 4 of the 51 pithas in the Shakta tradition.

nearestAirport.name: Lokpriya Gopinath Bordoloi International Airport, Guwahati

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kamakhya, Guwahati
topPlaces.description: Kamakhya is a locality in Guwahati, Assam, which is 20 kilometres (12 mi) from the Guwahati Airport and about 6 km from Guwahati Railway Station, it also has own railway junction called Kamakhya Junction. Kamakhya is known for presence of ancient Kamakhya Temple in Nilachal pahar or hill.

topPlaces.name: Maligaon
topPlaces.description: Maligaon is a locality of Guwahati, Assam. The headquarters of Northeast Frontier Railway and Northeast Frontier Railway Stadium is situated there.

topPlaces.name: Northeast Frontier Railway Stadium
topPlaces.description: North-East Frontier Railway Stadium is a multi-purpose stadium at Maligaon in Guwahati, Assam. It is mainly used for organizing matches of football and cricket.

topPlaces.name: Kendriya Vidyalaya Maligaon
topPlaces.description: Kendriya Vidyalaya Maligaon is a higher secondary school located at the Maligaon area in Guwahati, Assam, India. It was established in 1979 and is affiliated with the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE).

topPlaces.name: Silagrant
topPlaces.description: Silagrant also known as Sila Grant is a census village in North-Guwahati of Kamrup Metropolitan district, Assam, India. As per 2011 Census of India, the village has population of total 6,504 people, out of which 3,271 are males and 3,233 are females.

topPlaces.name: Atal Udyan
topPlaces.description: Atal Udyan (Assamese pronunciation: [ɔtɔl uˈdjan]) is an urban park situated at Adabari Tiniali in the Maligaon area of Guwahati, India.

topPlaces.name: Kumar Bhaskar Varma Setu
topPlaces.description: Kumar Bhaskar Varma Setu is a six-lane road bridge over the Brahmaputra River in Assam, India. It connects Guwahati with North Guwahati, significantly enhancing transportation and regional connectivity in the Northeast India.

topPlaces.name: Pandu, Guwahati
topPlaces.description: Pandu is a locality in Guwahati, India. Its nearest airport is Guwahati Airport and nearest railway station is the Kamakhya station.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kamakhya Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kamakhya Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kamakhya Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Lokpriya Gopinath Bordoloi International Airport, Guwahati (14 km) and the nearest railway station is Guwahati Railway Station (5 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kamakhya Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kamakhya Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kamakhya Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kamakhya Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kamakhya Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kamakhya, Guwahati, Maligaon, Northeast Frontier Railway Stadium, Kendriya Vidyalaya Maligaon, Silagrant.
